tasks.storeRelativePath.description: Relative path (from workspace root) to legacy task JSON used only by migrate-task-persistence when importing old files.

tasks.persistenceBackend.description: Task + wishlist runtime persistence: sqlite only (unified planning DB).

tasks.sqliteDatabaseRelativePath.description: Relative path (from workspace root) to the SQLite file when persistenceBackend is sqlite.

tasks.strictValidation.description: When true, task mutations validate the full active task set before persistence and fail on invalid task records.

tasks.planningGenerationPolicy.description: How strictly mutating task-engine / planning-store commands must pass expectedPlanningGeneration for SQLite optimistic concurrency: off (optional token, last-writer-wins when omitted), warn (omit allowed but response may include planningGenerationPolicyWarnings), require (omit fails with planning-generation-required). Published default is off; maintainer repos often set require.

modules.enabled.description: When non-empty, only these module ids are enabled (whitelist); then modules.disabled subtracts. When empty, all modules use registration.enabledByDefault.

modules.disabled.description: Module ids to disable after computing the candidate enabled set (default-by-flag or modules.enabled whitelist).

kit.currentPhaseNumber.description: Optional positive integer marking the maintainer’s current kit phase number. When set, queue-health and phase hints prefer this over parsing docs/maintainers/data/workspace-kit-status.yaml. Must agree with that YAML when both are set (workspace-kit doctor warns on mismatch).

kit.currentPhaseLabel.description: Optional human-readable phase label (for explain-config / operator context); does not replace task.phase strings.

kit.agentGuidance.profileSetId.description: Agent guidance catalog id. v1: rpg_party_v1 (RPG party tier labels). Advisory only — subordinate to PRINCIPLES and policy.

kit.agentGuidance.tier.description: Interaction difficulty tier 1–5 (NPC → BBEG) for advisory agent guidance; see ADR-agent-guidance-profile-rpg-party-v1.md.

kit.agentGuidance.displayLabel.description: Optional echo of the user-facing label chosen during onboarding (e.g. Bard); does not drive validation — tier is authoritative.

agentBehavior.activeProfileId.description: Active behavior profile id (e.g. builtin:balanced, custom:slug). Empty string means fall back to builtin default. Mirrored with SQLite module state when present.

agentBehavior.customProfiles.description: Custom behavior profiles keyed by custom:<slug>; merged with builtins at resolve time. Mirrored with SQLite when using unified DB.

planning.defaultQuestionDepth.description: Planning interview depth mode: minimal (critical only), guided (critical + static follow-ups), or adaptive (context-driven follow-ups).

planning.hardBlockCriticalUnknowns.description: When true, planning finalize requests fail until critical unknown questions are answered.

planning.adaptiveFinalizePolicy.description: Controls finalize handling for unresolved adaptive follow-up questions: off (ignore), warn (allow finalize with warnings), block (deny finalize).

planning.rulePacks.description: Optional object overrides for planning rule packs by workflow type (`baseQuestions` and `adaptiveQuestions`).

policy.extraSensitiveModuleCommands.description: Additional module command names (e.g. run subcommands) treated as sensitive for policy approval.

improvement.transcripts.sourcePath.description: Optional relative path to transcript JSONL source. When empty, sync uses discoveryPaths (repo-relative, then Cursor global ~/.cursor/projects/<slug>/agent-transcripts).

improvement.transcripts.archivePath.description: Relative local archive path where synced transcript JSONL files are copied.

improvement.cadence.minIntervalMinutes.description: Minimum minutes between one-shot ingest recommendation generation runs.

improvement.cadence.skipIfNoNewTranscrip.description: When true, skip the ingest-time recommendation generation step if transcript sync copied no new files; set false to still score policy/diff/task-transition evidence on a cadence.

improvement.cadence.maxRecommendationCan.description: Upper bound on new improvement tasks created per generate-recommendations run (safety cap; direct runs still respect dedupe).

improvement.transcripts.maxFilesPerSync.description: Maximum JSONL transcript files processed per sync (deterministic order).

improvement.transcripts.maxBytesPerFile.description: Skip transcript files larger than this many bytes during sync.

improvement.transcripts.maxTotalScanByte.description: Approximate cap on total bytes read for hashing during one sync.

improvement.transcripts.discoveryPaths.description: Ordered relative paths tried when improvement.transcripts.sourcePath is unset (first existing wins). After these, sync tries Cursor global ~/.cursor/projects/<slug>/agent-transcripts.

improvement.recommendations.heuristicVer.description: Improvement ingest admission heuristic: 1 (default, max-of-signals) or 2 (mean-of-signals with alternate threshold).

improvement.hooks.afterTaskCompleted.description: Optional background transcript sync after task-engine transition to completed: off (default), sync, or ingest (ingest requires WORKSPACE_KIT_POLICY_APPROVAL in env).

responseTemplates.enforcementMode.description: `advisory`: unknown template ids, invalid default/override ids, and explicit-vs-directive template conflicts emit warnings only. `strict`: same conditions fail the command (`response-template-invalid` or `response-template-conflict`) after the module runs; use for CI governance.

responseTemplates.defaultTemplateId.description: Builtin response template id applied when a run does not specify one.

responseTemplates.commandOverrides.description: Map of module command name to builtin response template id.

skills.discoveryRoots.description: Workspace-relative directories scanned for skill packs (Claude-shaped: <root>/<skill-id>/SKILL.md).

plugins.discoveryRoots.description: Workspace-relative directories scanned for Claude Code–layout plugins: <root>/<plugin>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json.

kit.githubInvocation.enabled.description: When true, the reference GitHub delivery runner may spawn workspace-kit for allowed repositories (still requires allowlist + signature or trusted Actions context). Default false: no remote invocation.

kit.githubInvocation.allowedRepositories.description: Full repository names (owner/repo) permitted for GitHub-native invocation. Empty = deny all remote runs.

kit.githubInvocation.eventPlaybookMap.description: Maps GitHub event name (e.g. issue_comment, pull_request_review) to route kind: plan, implement, review, fix-review, or none. Slash commands in the comment body override this map. See ADR-github-native-invocation.md.

kit.githubInvocation.commentDebounceSeco.description: Minimum seconds between automated runner actions for the same issue/PR thread (in-process only; multi-replica setups need external coordination). 0 disables debounce.

kit.githubInvocation.rateLimitEventsPerH.description: Placeholder for future rate limiting: max automated invocations per rolling hour per repo (0 = not enforced by reference runner).

kit.githubInvocation.planOnlyRunCommands.description: workspace-kit run subcommand names allowed for plan route (e.g. get-next-actions, list-tasks, get-task). Runner rejects plan invocations outside this list.

kit.githubInvocation.sensitiveRunCommand.description: workspace-kit run subcommand names permitted for implement/review routes when WORKSPACE_KIT_GITHUB_RUN_ARGS_JSON + WORKSPACE_KIT_GITHUB_RUN_POLICY_APPROVAL are supplied (maintainer-controlled JSON).

kit.lifecycleHooks.enabled.description: When true, registered kit.lifecycleHooks.handlers may run for configured lifecycle events (see ADR-agent-task-lifecycle-hooks-v1.md).

kit.lifecycleHooks.mode.description: Hook posture: off (disabled), observe (handlers run; deny/modify logged only), enforce (deny stops work; modify merges allowed patches).

kit.lifecycleHooks.traceRelativePath.description: Workspace-relative append-only JSONL trace path for hook invocations (no secrets; see lifecycle hooks runbook).

kit.autoCheckpoint.enabled.description: When true, workspace-kit run may create a checkpoint before commands listed in kit.autoCheckpoint.beforeCommands (fail-closed on errors; see ADR-task-linked-checkpoints-v1.md).

kit.autoCheckpoint.beforeCommands.description: `workspace-kit run` subcommand names that trigger an auto-checkpoint when kit.autoCheckpoint.enabled is true (default includes run-transition).

kit.autoCheckpoint.stashWhenDirty.description: When true (default), dirty worktrees use git stash for auto-checkpoints; when false, dirty worktrees fail auto-checkpoint until clean.
